pairs.question: Who was Clarence William Edward James Fleay and what is he known for in cricket?
pairs.answer: Clarence William Edward James Fleay was an Australian cricketer who played only one first-class match for Western Australia during the 1922–23 season. He is known for his brief but notable appearance in first-class cricket and his contributions to district cricket in Western Australia.

pairs.question: What was Clarence Fleay's highest score in school cricket, and during which season?
pairs.answer: Clarence Fleay's highest score in school cricket was an innings of 137 not out, achieved during the 1905–06 season while playing for The High School (now Hale School) in Perth.

pairs.question: How did Clarence Fleay get selected for the Western Australia first-class team?
pairs.answer: Clarence Fleay was selected for the Western Australia first-class team based on his strong performance in the preceding year's Country Week matches. His selection was notable as he was one of only two players chosen from country sides, the other being Joe Lanigan.

pairs.question: What were Clarence Fleay's bowling figures in his only first-class match?
pairs.answer: In his only first-class match against the Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) in November 1922, Clarence Fleay took two wickets in the MCC's first innings, finishing with bowling figures of 2/10.

pairs.question: Where and when did Clarence Fleay pass away?
pairs.answer: Clarence Fleay passed away in Katanning, Western Australia, in August 1955 at the age of 68.

pairs.question: What role did Clarence Fleay primarily play in cricket, and how did he contribute to his school and district teams?
pairs.answer: Clarence Fleay was an occasional right-arm medium pace bowler. During his schooling at The High School (now Hale School), he was one of the school's most successful batsmen, while in district cricket, he regularly represented Katanning at the Country Week tournament and sometimes captained the side.
